goal: Stop the user-added Workspace.Ball from dropping by making submarine float forces cover the full constrained ball assembly.
root_cause: The first float pass attached buoyancy primarily to the exterior CollisionBall.
Workspace.Ball is made of several physical parts across constrained assemblies, including exterior glass/rings, interior body pieces, and a driver seat.
The runtime server path also attempted to write Script.Source, which can fail without PluginOrOpenCloud capability.
changes: Added multi-part submarine float version marker: multi_part_v2.
Added per-physical-part VectorForce bodies using each part's own mass instead of a single assembly force.
Added a builder-owned RunService.Heartbeat controller so runtime float does not depend on writing a new Script.Source.
Guarded optional Script.Source assignment with pcall for Studio/plugin contexts.
Set all ball physical descendants unanchored and non-massless so mass and lift are coherent.
Kept old rolling angular/align controls disabled to prevent the floating ball from being pulled by the previous rolling controller.
Added live telemetry attributes: SubmarineFloatBodyCount, SubmarineFloatTotalMass, and SubmarineFloatTotalForceY.
validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio with the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() in Studio and confirmed the build returned CellEnvironment_Main.
Queried Workspace.Ball in Studio and confirmed seven active buoyancy forces.
result: The ball now has enough lift applied across all physical pieces to rise instead of dropping, with runtime telemetry showing the heartbeat controller is active.
residual_risks: Full play-mode physics observation through the legacy bridge is limited because the official MCP helper intermittently occupies the Studio polling port.
If the ball should become player-drivable later, the rolling AngularVelocity package controls should be replaced with submarine thrust controls instead of re-enabled.
